claimText: The Montreal Protocol is a legally binding treaty implemented under the 1985 Vienna Convention to eliminate Ozone Depleting Substances.
evidenceExcerpt: The Montreal Protocol (signed 1987, implemented under the 1985 Vienna Convention) is a legally binding treaty to eliminate Ozone Depleting Substances (ODS) like CFCs and Halons.
distractorHooks: Implemented under UNFCCC or Kyoto Protocol

claimText: The 2016 Kigali Amendment targets the phase-down of Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs), which are potent greenhouse gases but not ozone-depleting substances.
evidenceExcerpt: The 2016 Kigali Amendment aims to phase down HFCs, which are non-ODS but potent greenhouse gases.
distractorHooks: Kigali Amendment is under the Paris Agreement

claimText: Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) are short-lived climate pollutants with an average atmospheric lifetime of 15 years that have a significantly higher warming potential than CO2 over a 20-year period.
evidenceExcerpt: HFCs are short-lived climate pollutants with an average atmospheric lifetime of 15 years, warming the planet 3,790 times as much as CO2 over 20 years.
examUse: Statement-based question on greenhouse gas properties.
distractorHooks: HFCs have lower warming potential than CO2

claimText: The ozone layer is projected to recover to 1980 values by 2040 globally, with delayed recovery timelines for the Arctic (2045) and Antarctic (2066).
evidenceExcerpt: The ozone layer is expected to recover to 1980 values by 2040 globally, 2045 over the Arctic, and 2066 over the Antarctic.
examUse: Statement-based question on environmental recovery milestones.
distractorHooks: Antarctic ozone will recover before the global average

claimText: India successfully phased out CFCs, Carbon Tetrachloride (CTC), and halons for controlled use by 2010, ahead of the Montreal Protocol schedules.
evidenceExcerpt: India phased out CFCs, Carbon Tetrachloride (CTC), and halons for controlled use by 2010, ahead of Montreal Protocol schedules.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's environmental commitments.
distractorHooks: India missed the Montreal Protocol deadline
